---
title: "Three group control analysis"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-01"
---

# Steps can be repeated for Replogle

```{r}
# Loading the library and creating the subset:

library(anndata)
library(reticulate)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
py_require("scanpy")
scanpy <- import("scanpy")
py_require("scanpy")
scanpy <- import("scanpy")

obj <- read_h5ad("data/TianKampmann2021_CRISPRi.h5ad")  
scanpy$pp$normalize_total(obj)  # Normalize: Each cell's counts sum to 10,000, Each cell's counts sum to 10,000
scanpy$pp$log1p(obj)  # Log transform: log(x 1) transformation, Makes data more normal/distributed

# selecting highly variable genes
scanpy$pp$highly_variable_genes(obj, n_top_genes=5000L)
# Subset to highly variable genes

ad_sub <- obj[, obj$var$highly_variable]
X_sub <- ad_sub$X

dim(X_sub)  # Check dimensions of the subsetted data
meta_sub <- as.data.frame(ad_sub$obs)

ad_sub$X[1:5, 1:5] 
```
# Boxplot with one control centroid
```{r}
# ============================================================
# EXPERIMENT 1: VARY CONTROL SAMPLE SIZE
# Keep perturbed cells fixed at n_pert = 50
# ============================================================

set.seed(42)

# Get control matrix
ctrl_idx <- which(ad_sub$obs$perturbation == "control")
X_ctrl <- X_sub[ctrl_idx, ] |> as.matrix()
n_ctrl_total <- nrow(X_ctrl)

# Fixed number of perturbed cells
n_pert <- 50

# Vary control sample size
control_sizes <- c(10, 25, 50, 100, 200)
n_reps <- 30  # replicates per condition

# Store results
results_control_size <- data.frame()

for(n_control in control_sizes) {
  
  # Skip if not enough control cells
  if(n_control > n_ctrl_total) next
  
  for(rep in 1:n_reps) {
    
    # Sample control cells (for control group)
    control_idx <- sample(n_ctrl_total, n_control)
    ctrl_centroid <- colMeans(X_ctrl[control_idx, , drop = FALSE])
    
    # Sample DIFFERENT control cells (for fake perturbation 1)
    pert1_idx <- sample(setdiff(1:n_ctrl_total, control_idx), n_pert)
    pert1_centroid <- colMeans(X_ctrl[pert1_idx, , drop = FALSE])
    
    # Sample DIFFERENT control cells (for fake perturbation 2)
    used_idx <- c(control_idx, pert1_idx)
    pert2_idx <- sample(setdiff(1:n_ctrl_total, used_idx), n_pert)
    pert2_centroid <- colMeans(X_ctrl[pert2_idx, , drop = FALSE])
    
    # Calculate shifts
    shift1 <- pert1_centroid - ctrl_centroid
    shift2 <- pert2_centroid - ctrl_centroid
    
    # Cosine similarity
    #cos_val <- sum(shift1 * shift2) / (sqrt(sum(shift1^2)) * sqrt(sum(shift2^2)) + 1e-8)
    cos_val <- cor(shift1, shift2)
    
    results_control_size <- rbind(results_control_size, 
                                   data.frame(n_control = n_control, 
                                              n_pert = n_pert,
                                              replicate = rep,
                                              cosine = cos_val))
  }
}

# Plot results
library(ggplot2)

control_boxplot <- ggplot(results_control_size, aes(x = factor(n_control), y = cosine)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Effect of Control Sample Size on Correlation",
       x = "Number of Control Cells",
       y = "Correlation") +
  theme_bw()
  #theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Summary statistics
aggregate(cosine ~ n_control, results_control_size, 
          function(x) c(mean = mean(x), sd = sd(x)))

print(control_boxplot)
ggsave("control_boxplot.png", control_boxplot, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```
